own_review.file: runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/tracing/intrumentation/ (17 renamed files + 6 import sites in OpenTelemetryProcessor.java & TracerProcessor.java & TracerRecorder.java)
own_review.issue: Package-name typo 'intrumentation' (missing second 'e') systematically introduced across all four instrumentation subpackages (grpc, vertx, restclient, reactivemessaging). Consistent so it compiles, but it is a permanent public package name baked into a 'pure refactor' that claims to improve clarity. Central defect.

own_review.file: runtime tracing.intrumentation.* vs deployment test instrumentation.*
own_review.issue: Internal inconsistency: runtime package is misspelled 'intrumentation' while the moved deployment test package is correctly spelled 'instrumentation' (io.quarkus.opentelemetry.deployment.instrumentation). The two halves of the same refactor diverge.

own_review.file: PR stated goal vs runtime package naming
own_review.issue: PR claims to 'align with the packages used on the Micrometer extension'. Micrometer uses runtime.binder.{grpc,vertx,kafka,...}; this PR uses runtime.tracing.(in)strumentation.*. The naming does NOT actually align with Micrometer's 'binder' convention (the human's 'binder vs intrumenter' comment).

gRPC proto-generated classes keep java_package=io.quarkus.opentelemetry.deployment while the test moved to ...deployment.instrumentation. Compiles via explicit imports, but the proto java_package was NOT aligned to the new test subpackage — the human's actual requested follow-up.
resourceAttributes field visibility widened package-private -> public during a 'pure refactor'; an undocumented public-surface change.

isClassPresent removal is SAFE: it was a thin wrapper over QuarkusClassLoader.isClassPresentAtRuntime; sole caller TracerProcessor switches to a direct static import of isClassPresentAtRuntime (which exists in base). Any claim this breaks TracerProcessor would be fabricated.

points.note: Central defect correctly identified; notes propagation to 4 subpackages + imports, confirmed newly introduced (absent at base), and correctly observes Micrometer has no 'instrumentation' package.
Real change verified (package-private -> public at TracerRuntimeConfig:20); reasonably asks to confirm intent.
Correctly describes removal as a clean simplification, sole caller TracerProcessor switches to direct call, no other callers. Verified true.

missed: Human's proto-generated gRPC java_package alignment follow-up (protos still java_package=io.quarkus.opentelemetry.deployment, not aligned to the new instrumentation test subpackage).
exceeds: Independently confirmed the typo is absent from the base commit (newly introduced).

points.note: Central defect correctly identified; explicitly notes propagation into imports in OpenTelemetryProcessor and TracerProcessor and across ~20 files.
Correctly: pass-through wrapper, TracerProcessor now calls QuarkusClassLoader.isClassPresentAtRuntime directly, no other callers. Verified.
Real change verified; correctly notes Quarkus config reflection convention makes it appropriate.

Correct: moved GrpcOpenTelemetryTest adds explicit imports for proto-generated classes that remain in deployment package and resolve. Sits on the gRPC/proto area the human cared about, though stops short of recommending proto alignment.
Verified true: no leftover references to old opentelemetry package paths anywhere in extensions; refactor is internally consistent / compiles.

missed: Human's proto-generated gRPC java_package alignment follow-up (noted imports resolve but did NOT recommend aligning the proto java_package).

Micrometer 'binder' naming divergence: summary asserts 'align with Micrometer conventions' but never flags that runtime.tracing.instrumentation.* does not match Micrometer's runtime.binder.* convention.
exceeds: Explicitly verified self-containment across integration-tests, test-framework, independent-projects, and other extensions (correct).

qualitative: Both reviews nail the central defect (the systematic 'intrumentation' typo) as a blocker and both correctly characterize the isClassPresent removal as a safe simplification rather than inventing a TracerProcessor breakage — a trap both avoided. Every concrete claim in both texts is verifiable against the repo: the resourceAttributes visibility widening is real, and v8's self-containment assertion was confirmed by grep (zero leftover old-package references), so absence-fabrications are zero for both. v8 is the stronger review: it adds two more accurate, relevant points (the gRPC-test proto-import resolution and verified self-containment) without introducing any falsehoods, edging ahead of v7. Both miss the human's actual follow-up request to align the gRPC proto java_package with the new test subpackage, and v8 additionally under-delivers on the Micrometer 'binder' naming divergence that its own summary implicitly raises. v8 is a from-scratch Docker rebuild of the v7 harness and behaves as intended, producing a superset of v7's findings with equal accuracy.
